name: dev
description: Enforced dev workflow (/dev + /spec + /prd) with dual-model review, independent evaluator architecture, AC-based module progress formula, aligned §N.M section numbering, git auto-sync with LLM-generated commit messages, opt-in statusline, `/spec upgrade-template` for template migration preserving /dev verification progress, and `/prd` skill for iterative PRD generation with 4-dimension coverage evaluator. MODULE template gains §1.1 Serves PRD topics, §2.13 Operations, §2.14 Observability. Adds /dev PLAN routing via CONTEXT-MAP + domain-term resolution via GLOSSARY (auto-generated by /spec and /prd). **2.5.0** adds ADR (Architecture Decision Records) system: `/spec adr-new` subcommand + inline ADR template, Phase 1.0 conflict detection over Accepted ADRs (22 opposing-keyword pairs + decision-marker proximity), /dev PLAN ADR compliance block, /dev DOCS ADR check (abort+restart). **2.6.0** tightens /prd scope discipline: Phase 4 Dimension 4 adds architecture-leakage detection (module IDs / DDL / API route tables / trait signatures / cross-module diagrams / directory layouts flagged as Critical), plus optional PRD template sub-sections §1.1 Design principles (cross-cutting product invariants) and §7.1 Milestones / rollout (high-level user-capability roadmap, distinct from /spec IMPLEMENTATION_ORDER.md), plus Phase 3.0 boundary block separating PRD-scope from /spec-scope content. **2.7.0** adds upstream-alignment escape hatches so the prd → spec → dev pipeline can flow fixes upstream without silent document drift: /dev DOCS §2.1.2 (PRD/Spec upstream change check — abort+restart mirroring §2.1.1 ADR), §2.1.3 (Core Logic drift check — re-entry-only §2.7 alignment audit), and /spec Phase 0.6 (PRD-gap escalation — formalizes the /spec Iron Rule upstream-rollback path as a structured 3-option AskUserQuestion).
